title: lupinus arizonicus var. barbatulus
intro: Lupinus arizonicus var. barbatulus, also known as Arizona lupine, is a variety of lupine plant that belongs to the legume family and is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Arizona lupine include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve heart health, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by drying the plant to create a powder that can be added to supplements or capsules.
